raw.title: Unreinforced CMU Bearing Wall — ASD
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse Unreinforced CMU Bearing Wall — ASD 4″ & 6″ Nominal CMU · Combined Axial + Wind Flexure · Allowable Stress Design per TMS 402-22 TMS 402-22 Chapter 8 §8.2.4 Allowable Axial §8.2.5 Flexural Tension §8.2.6 Combined Loading MDG-2022 WALL SCHEMATIC w = 5.0 psf P = 2.0 k/ft δ h = 10.0' t = 3.625" Top support Btm. support Elevation hollow core t = 3.625" 12" (1 ft) tfs = 0.75" 4" nom. CMU (hollow) Cross Section (per ft) ■ Wall Type & Material TMS 402-22 §8.2 Nominal Wall Thickness 4″ Nominal CMU (actual 3.625″) 6″ Nominal CMU (actual 5.625″) Actual t = 3.625″ · An = 18 in²/ft · S = 21 in³/ft · r = 1.047″ Grout Condition Hollow (Ungrouted) Fully Grouted Face-shell bedding for hollow; gross section for grouted Specified Masonry Strength f′m (psi) Typical: 1500–2000 psi for CMU Mortar Type Type S Type N Affects Ft per TMS 402-22 Table 8.2.5.2 ■ Geometry & Loading ASD Load Combination Wall Height h (ft) Clear height between supports Max ASD Vertical Load P (plf) Total axial load at critical section (lb per ft of wall). For tension check, re-run with min P. Interior Wind Pressure w (psf) Lateral pressure on wall face Support Conditions Simply Supported — M = wh²/8 Fixed-Pinned — M = wh²/10 Fixed-Fixed — M = wh²/12 Most conservative and common: simply supported Apply ⅓ stress increase for wind load combination (TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.1) — multiply allowable stresses by 4/3 ▶ Run All Checks ✗ ONE OR MORE CHECKS FAIL Governing D/C = 1.66 · 4″ CMU — Hollow (Ungrouted) · h = 10.0 ft · fʼm = 1500.0 psi · P = 2000.0 plf · w = 5.0 psf · ⅓ wind increase applied SECTION PROPERTIES — PER FOOT OF WALL Property Symbol Value Notes Nominal thickness tnom 4″ Actual thickness t 3.6 in CMU modular dimension Net area An 18.0 in²/ft Face-shell bedding: 2 × t₟s × 12 Net section modulus Sn 21.0 in³/ft Iₙ/(t/2) Net moment of inertia In 38.0 in⁴/ft t³ − (t−2t₟s)³ Radius of gyration r 1.0 in t/√12 (simplified per TMS) Face shell thickness tfs 0.8 in Min. per TMS 602-22 Wall weight wwall 34.0 psf Approximate h/r slenderness h/r 114.6 Slender: use Eq. (8-17) h/t slenderness h/t 33.1 Exceeds 20 — check TMS requirement KEY DEMANDS & ALLOWABLES 111.1 psi Actual Axial Stress fₐ = P/Aₙ 186.5 psi Allow. Axial Stress Fₐ (eff.) 62.5 ft‑lb/ft Wind Moment at Mid-Height Mₘₐˣ 35.7 psi Actual Flexural Stress fᵇ 660.0 psi Allow. Flexural Stress Fᵇ (eff.) 0.65 Combined Unity fₐ/Fₐ + fᵇ/Fᵇ -75.4 psi Net Flexural Tension fᵇ − fₐ 33.3 psi Allow. Flex. Tension Fₜ (eff.) CODE CHECKS Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Slenderness — h/t Max h/t = 20 for unreinforced bearing walls TMS 402-22 §5.5.1.1 h/t = 33.1 Limit = 20 1.66 FAIL ▶ Calc Axial Stress fa vs Fa Fₐ base = 139.9 psi; ×4/3 for wind combo TMS 402-22 §8.2.4.3 fa = 111.1 psi Fa = 186.5 psi (incl. ⅓) 0.60 PASS ▶ Calc Flexural Stress fb vs Fb M = 62.5 ft-lb/ft (w = 5.0 psf, h = 10.0 ft) TMS 402-22 §8.2.3 fb = 35.7 psi Fb = 660.0 psi (incl. ⅓) 0.05 PASS ▶ Calc Combined Compression — Unity Check fₐ/Fₐ = 0.596 + fᵇ/Fᵇ = 0.054 TMS 402-22 §8.2.6.2 fa Fa + fb Fb ≤ 1.0 0.65 PASS ▶ Calc Net Flexural Tension (fb − fa) vs Ft Gravity compression exceeds wind tension TMS 402-22 §8.2.5 fnet = -75.4 psi (compression) N/A — no tension N/A PASS ▶ Calc Engineering Notes & Assumptions: All calculations per TMS 402-22 (Building Code Requirements and Specification for Masonry Structures) and MDG-2022. Section properties use r = t/√12 (simplified, slightly conservative) per standard ASD masonry practice. Hollow (ungrouted) area based on face-shell bedding only: Aₙ = 2 × t₟s × 12 in²/ft. Net I and S based on hollow box section. P is the total ASD vertical load (dead + applicable live) at the critical section. Run a separate check with minimum P (0.6D only) to evaluate the tension case under wind. 4/3 stress increase applied per TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.1 for D+W or D+L+W load combinations. Moment assumes wall simply supported at top and bottom for M = wh²/8. Verify boundary conditions for your project. Self-weight of wall is NOT automatically added to P. Include it as part of the input P if required at the critical section. TMS 402-22 Table 8.2.5.2 Fₜ values are for in-plane running bond, normal to bed joints (bending about horizontal axis as occurs here). This calculator checks out-of-plane bending only. In-plane shear and axial load to be checked separately per TMS 402-22 Chapter 8. For walls with large openings, pilasters, or non-uniform loading, additional analysis is required beyond this uniform-wall check. ⇄ Results Hub ⇄ Results Hub×
